name: validation
title: Validation
description: A live requirements checklist (USWDS "validation") — e.g. password rules that tick off as the user types. Each item pairs a non-color met/unmet indicator (check vs open circle) with a visually-hidden status word, and the list is a polite live region so items announce as they flip.
useWhen: Showing rules a value must satisfy and updating them live as the user types (password, username)
Giving inline, per-requirement pass/fail feedback instead of one lump error
Helping users succeed on the first submit by making requirements visible up front
avoidWhen: A single pass/fail error after submit (use the Field error live region)
Requirements that cannot be checked client-side (server-only rules)
Static, non-interactive lists of guidance (use a normal list)

keyboard: Adds no keyboard behavior of its own; the checklist is not interactive
Focus stays on the input the checklist describes
accessibility.notes: The list is a polite live region (aria-live="polite") so each item announces as it flips between met and unmet without moving focus. Every item pairs its color with a distinct shape (checkmark vs open circle, inline SVG currentColor, aria-hidden) plus a visually-hidden status word ("met"/"not met", via statusLabels) so state is never color-only (WCAG 1.4.1) and survives forced-colors mode. The region is named via a visible heading (aria-labelledby) or the label prop (aria-label); associate it with the input by adding its id to the input's aria-describedby.
